channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.118
item.description: Learn what's new in Visual Studio Code 1.118 (Insiders) Read the full article
item.dc:creator: Visual Studio Code Team

item.title: Notice about upcoming new format for GitHub App installation tokens
item.description: Starting April 27th 2026 and over the coming weeks, we will begin a staged rollout that updates the format of newly minted GitHub App installation tokens, making them more performant… The post Notice about upcoming new format for GitHub App installation tokens appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Azure MCP Server now available as an MCP Bundle (.mcpb)
item.description: Azure MCP Server is now available as an MCP Bundle (.mcpb), enabling one-click installation into Claude Desktop and other MCP-compatible clients. The post Azure MCP Server now available as an MCP Bundle (.mcpb) appeared first on Azure SDK Blog.
item.dc:creator: Victor Colin Amador

item.title: Changes to notification retention and archived repository watches
item.description: Two updates will change the way GitHub notifications and repository watches are retained. These updates are rolling out soon, and are expected to be completed over the next few months.… The post Changes to notification retention and archived repository watches appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Kubernetes v1.36: Fine-Grained Kubelet API Authorization Graduates to GA
item.description: On behalf of Kubernetes SIG Auth and SIG Node, we are pleased to announce the graduation of fine-grained kubelet API authorization to General Availability (GA) in Kubernetes v1.36! The KubeletFineGrainedAuthz feature gate was introduced as an opt-in alpha feature in Kubernetes v1.32, then graduated...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Backup for Elastic SAN
item.description: Azure Backup now supports Elastic SAN, offering a fully managed solution for backing up and restoring Elastic SAN volumes. This integration helps protect data against accidental deletions, ransomware attacks, and application updates by exporting Elastic S
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Hosted Agents in Foundry Agent Service
item.description: Hosted agents run in an isolated execution sandbox that provides a dedicated, secure runtime per agent session. Sessions start with a clean environment with no shared state between sessions, no cross-session data leakage, and strong compute boundaries bet
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Foundry Toolkit for Visual Studio Code (formerly AI Toolkit for VS Code)
item.description: Foundry Toolkit for VS Code is now generally available and provides VS Code tooling for Microsoft Agent Framework, including agent creation from templates or via GitHub Copilot, local test/debug with visualization and traces, and direct deployment to Foun
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: WireGuard In‑Transit Encryption for Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)
item.description: WireGuard in‑transit encryption is now generally available for Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) clusters using Azure CNI powered by Cilium and Advanced Container Networking Services (ACNS). This capability enables transparent, node‑level encryption for inte
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Retirement: Support for .NET 8 (LTS) ends on November 10, 2026—upgrade your apps to .NET 10 (LTS)
item.description: On November 10, 2026, support for .NET 8 (LTS) will end. Your apps that are hosted on App Service will continue to run, but security updates will no longer be available and we'll no longer provide customer service for .NET 8 (LTS). Required action: To avo
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: GPT-5.5 is generally available for GitHub Copilot
item.description: GPT-5.5, OpenAI’s latest GPT model, is now rolling out on GitHub Copilot. In our early testing, GPT-5.5 delivers its strongest performance on complex, multi-step agentic coding task and resolves real-world… The post GPT-5.5 is generally available for GitHub Copilot appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Cascading read replicas in Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description: Cascading read replicas are now generally available in Azure Database for PostgreSQL, giving you greater flexibility to scale read‑intensive workloads. You can now create read replicas from existing ones, creating up to 30 across two levels of replication
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: CRC Protection
item.description: Azure Elastic SAN now supports CRC-32C checksum verification when enabled on the client side for connections to Elastic SAN volumes. You can enable CRC protection when creating an Elastic SAN or enable it on an existing Elastic SAN. Elastic SAN also offer
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Inline agent mode in preview and more in GitHub Copilot for JetBrains IDEs
item.description: This update introduces inline agent mode in preview, enhancements to Next Edit Suggestions, global auto approve, and more flexible controls for terminal commands and file edits. It also includes several… The post Inline agent mode in preview and more in GitHub Copilot for JetBrains IDEs appeared...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Axios npm Supply Chain Compromise – Guidance for Azure Pipelines Customers
item.description: On March 31, 2026, malicious versions of the widely used JavaScript HTTP client library Axios were briefly published to the npm registry as part of a supply chain attack. The affected versions — 1.14.1 and 0.30.4 — included a hidden malicious dependency that executed during installation and...
item.dc:creator: Josef Sin

item.title: Copilot Chat improvements for pull requests
item.description: GitHub Copilot Chat now provides richer context and new capabilities when you’re working with diffs and pull requests. You can access this functionality by asking a question about a pull… The post Copilot Chat improvements for pull requests appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Global pull requests dashboard moves to opt-out public preview
item.description: The new global pull requests dashboard will be on by default for all GitHub users as the new experience transitions to an opt-out public preview. The new dashboard and inbox… The post Global pull requests dashboard moves to opt-out public preview appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Kubernetes v1.36: User Namespaces in Kubernetes are finally GA
item.description: After several years of development, User Namespaces support in Kubernetes reached General Availability (GA) with the v1.36 release. This is a Linux-only feature. For those of us working on low level container runtimes and rootless technologies, this has been a long awaited milestone. We finally...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Capacity Autoscaling
item.description: Capacity Autoscaling support is now generally available on Elastic SAN. Customers can avoid the constraints of overprovisioning and manual capacity management by setting a policy to automatically expand SAN Capacity based on usage. Autoscaling provides th
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Copilot cloud agent fields added to usage metrics
item.description: Following the Copilot coding agent to Copilot cloud agent rename, the Copilot usage metrics API now includes a new used_copilot_cloud_agent field in user-level reports. This boolean field mirrors the existing… The post Copilot cloud agent fields added to usage metrics appeared first on The GitHub...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Dependabot-based dependency graphs for Python
item.description: Python projects will now see more complete and accurate transitive dependency trees in their dependency graphs and Software Bills of Materials (SBOMs). This feature is based on a new type… The post Dependabot-based dependency graphs for Python appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Disable commit comments across your organization
item.description: You can now disable commit comments at the organization level—no more manually updating every repository one by one. A few weeks ago, we added the ability to disable commit comments… The post Disable commit comments across your organization appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: What’s new for .NET in Ubuntu 26.04
item.description: Ubuntu 26.04 (Resolute Raccoon) ships today with .NET 10. Here's how to install .NET 10 from the archive, use the new -resolute container tags, build Native AOT apps, and install .NET 8 and 9 from the dotnet-backports PPA. The post What’s new for .NET in Ubuntu 26.04 appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: Richard Lander

item.title: LangChain.js for Beginners: A Free Course to Build Agentic AI Apps with JavaScript
item.description: Want to build AI agents with JavaScript that go beyond basic chat completions? Agents that reason, call tools, and pull from knowledge bases on their own? We put together a free, open source course to help you get there. LangChain.js for Beginners is 8 chapters and 70+ runnable TypeScript examples....
item.dc:creator: Yohan Lasorsa

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Monitor for Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes with OpenShift and Azure Red Hat OpenShift
item.description: Azure Monitor provides a complete set of services for monitoring the health and performance of different layers of your Kubernetes infrastructure and the applications that depend on it.Azure Monitor support for Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes with OpenShift
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Better debugging with GitHub Copilot on the web
item.description: GitHub Copilot Chat in github.com helps you get to the root cause of an error faster when you paste a stack trace. Copilot recognizes stack traces more reliably and will… The post Better debugging with GitHub Copilot on the web appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Lessons Learned from Building a Well-Matching Intelligence Layer
item.description: Key insights from the EDA and Ground Truth journey for solving well identity resolution in the Energy industry—challenges that extend to any entity matching problem across distributed systems. The post Lessons Learned from Building a Well-Matching Intelligence Layer appeared first on ISE Developer...
item.dc:creator: Emmeline Hoops, Oscar Fimbres, Kavitha Sreenath

item.title: Write azd hooks in Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, or .NET
item.description: Hooks are one of the most popular features in azd, and now you can write them in Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, or .NET, not just Bash and PowerShell. What’s new? The Azure Developer CLI (azd) hook system now supports four more languages beyond Bash and PowerShell. You can write hook scripts in...
item.dc:creator: Kristen Womack

item.title: 7 tips to optimize Azure Cosmos DB costs for AI and agentic workloads
item.description: AI apps and agentic workloads expose inefficiencies in your data layer faster than any previous generation of apps. You’re storing embeddings, serving low-latency retrieval, handling bursty traffic from chat and orchestration, and often operating across regions. Done right, Azure Cosmos DB can...
item.dc:creator: Michal Toiba

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Arc adds SQL Server on Azure Virtual Machines as a migration target
item.description: Azure Arc migration now supports SQL Server on Azure Virtual Machines as a migration target. You can migrate Arc-enabled SQL Server instances not only to Azure SQL Managed Instance but also to SQL Server running on Azure infrastructure—using the same unif
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: SELinux Volume Label Changes goes GA (and likely implications in v1.37)
item.description: If you run Kubernetes on Linux with SELinux in enforcing mode, plan ahead: a future release (anticipated to be v1.37) is expected to turn the SELinuxMount feature gate on by default. This makes volume setup faster for most workloads, but it can break applications that still depend on the older...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Optimizing Git policy management at scale
item.description: With just a single improvement in the REST API of Azure DevOps, we achieved a massive reduction in CPU usage and execution time when managing Git policies: 2x less CPU and 10-15x faster execution! This change is already available to all users of Azure DevOps, and it’s time to share a bit more...
item.dc:creator: Azat Galiev

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Database for PostgreSQL flexible server in Denmark East
item.description: You can now deploy Azure Database for PostgreSQL flexible server in the Denmark East Azure region. Learn more.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Dynamic data masking with Azure Cosmos DB
item.description: Dynamic data masking (DDM) is a server-side, policy-based security feature in Azure Cosmos DB that helps your organization protect sensitive data from unauthorized access. DDM dynamically masks data for nonprivileged users, ensuring that sensitive informa
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Logical replication slot sync Status metric for Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server
item.description: You can now monitor the synchronization status of your logical replication slots in Azure Database for PostgreSQL – Flexible Server using the new logical_replication_slot_sync_status metric, now in public preview. This Azure Monitor metric shows whether e
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Introducing Toolboxes in Foundry
item.description: Available in Public Preview Today Toolbox is a new way to curate, configure, and reuse tools across all of your AI agents without rewiring them every time from Foundry. Today, teams build agents across different frameworks and runtimes. Each agent often wires tools directly, with its own...
item.dc:creator: Linda Li, Maria Naggaga, Ronak Chokshi

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Premium SSD v2 for Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description: We are excited to announce the general availability of Premium SSD v2 for Azure Database for PostgreSQL flexible server. With Premium SSD v2, you can achieve up to four times higher IOPS, significantly lower latency, and better price-performance for I/O-i
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Migrating from virtual network-integrated to Private Endpoint–capable network configuration
item.description: You can now migrate your Azure Database for PostgreSQL server from a virtual network‑integrated deployment to a network configuration that supports Private Endpoint connectivity. If you originally deployed your server inside a virtual network but now want
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Enhanced Mirroring Azure Database for PostgreSQL in Microsoft Fabric
item.description: New capabilities make it easier to mirror real-world Azure Database for PostgreSQL workloads at scale in Microsoft Fabric and operate them with confidence.You can mirror native PostgreSQL data types such as JSON, JSONB and other commonly used types, so ri
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: From Local to Production: The Complete Developer Journey for Building, Composing, and Deploying AI Agents
item.description: When we launched Microsoft Agent Framework last October, we made a promise: building production-grade AI agents should feel as natural and structured as building any other software. Today, we’re delivering on that promise — with the v1.0 release of Microsoft Agent Framework and the general...
item.dc:creator: Takuto Higuchi, jeffhollan

item.title: Introducing the new hosted agents in Foundry Agent Service: secure, scalable compute built for agents
item.description: Agents are already transforming how developers solve problems. Whether it’s a coding agent that refactors your repo overnight, a research agent that synthesizes hundreds of documents into a brief, or an ops agent that monitors and remediates infrastructure — the pattern is clear. Developers are...
item.dc:creator: Takuto Higuchi, jeffhollan, Lakshmi Ramakrishnan

item.title: Securing MCP: A Control Plane for Agent Tool Execution
item.description: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is quickly becoming a common way for AI agents to discover and use tools. It provides a consistent interface to databases, APIs, file systems, and third-party services, which makes it easier to plug capabilities into agent workflows. However, MCP standardizes the...
item.dc:creator: Jack Batzner

item.title: AI-powered defense for an AI-accelerated threat landscape
item.description: Read how Microsoft is partnering with Anthropic and broader industry to use leading models, paired with our platforms and expertise, to turn AI-driven discovery into protection at scale. The post AI-powered defense for an AI-accelerated threat landscape appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Ales Holecek

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.117
item.description: Learn what's new in Visual Studio Code 1.117 Read the full article
item.dc:creator: Visual Studio Code Team

item.title: Public Preview: Actual Result for Manual Tests in Azure Test Plans
item.description: We’re excited to announce the public preview of the highly anticipated Actual Result (AR) feature for manual testing in Azure Test Plans! This feature has been one of the top requests of the community, and we’re thrilled to make it available for you. Why use the Actual Result feature? Manual...
item.dc:creator: Panagiotis Liaros

item.title: v1.15.0-rc3
item.description: 1.15.0-rc3 (April 22, 2026) NEW FEATURES: We now produce builds for Windows ARM64 (#32719) You can set a deprecated attribute on variable and output blocks to indicate that they are deprecated. This will produce warnings when passing in a value for a deprecated variable or when referencing a...
item.dc:creator: hc-github-team-es-release-engineering

item.title: Securing Argo CD with Microsoft Entra ID: A Step-by-Step Guide
item.description: Learn how to secure the Argo CD extension for AKS with Microsoft Entra ID using Terraform, workload identity, OIDC, RBAC, and policy-based access control.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Kubernetes v1.36: ハル (Haru)
item.description: Editors: Chad M. Crowell, Kirti Goyal, Sophia Ugochukwu, Swathi Rao, Utkarsh Umre Similar to previous releases, the release of Kubernetes v1.36 introduces new stable, beta, and alpha features. The consistent delivery of high-quality releases underscores the strength of our development cycle and the...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Azure SDK Release (April 2026)
item.description: Azure SDK releases every month. In this post, you'll find this month's highlights and release notes. The post Azure SDK Release (April 2026) appeared first on Azure SDK Blog.
item.dc:creator: Ronnie Geraghty

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Monitor pipeline
item.description: Azure Monitor pipeline is now generally available.Azure Monitor pipeline provides a centralized control point for telemetry ingestion and transformation, designed specifically for secure, high‑throughput, enterprise‑scale scenarios. It's built on open-sou
item.dc:creator: Microsoft
